mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, mechanically increasing equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: The unemployment rate crossing below 5.5 percent signals labor market normalization, which expands consumer spending capacity and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns average negative or statistically indistinguishable from zero when UNRATE drops below 5.5 percent across three consecutive quarters.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in housing starts and nonfarm payrolls indicate synchronized demand expansion, which lifts corporate earnings expectations and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive readings in HOUST and PAYEMS fail to produce S&P 500 forward returns exceeding the unconditional mean by more than 0.5 percent over the 60-day horizon.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during late-cycle expansion signals capacity overshoot and supply glut, which compresses profit margins and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases do not correlate with negative S&P 500 forward returns when the economy is explicitly in a late-cycle expansion phase defined by yield curve inversion.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in retail sales forecasts exceeding 2 percent indicates weakening consumer demand, causing immediate negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns remain positive in more than 60 percent of instances where RSAFS drops more than 2 percent month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent rises in the consumer price index and the unemployment rate signal stagflation, forcing restrictive monetary policy and equity devaluation over the subsequent 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Stagflationary data prints do not produce negative S&P 500 forward returns exceeding the baseline by more than 1 percent over the 60-day window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding 2.5 percent annualized sustains positive corporate earnings revisions, which mechanically drive positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: GDP growth above 2.5 percent fails to generate positive S&P 500 forward returns in more than 50 percent of observed quarters over a 5-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Strong monthly payroll growth exceeding 200,000 jobs during early-cycle recovery accelerates earnings revisions and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Payroll growth exceeding 200,000 jobs does not yield positive S&P 500 forward returns above the unconditional mean during early-cycle recovery phases.
